input:
- # Tine ⟷ OG parity test graph

A shared Logseq graph (standard `pages/` + `journals/` layout) for verifying that
Tine matches OG (original Logseq). **Open the same folder in both apps and compare.**

Convention: **every Tine feature or bug fix adds a small, self-contained example
here** — one page (or a few) that exercises the behavior, so we can eyeball it
side by side in OG and Tine and catch parity gaps. Keep examples minimal and
labeled by the feature they test.

**Reference screenshots live in `assets/`** so they survive across sessions:
`<feature>-og.png` is the oracle (Martin screenshots real OG), `<feature>-tine.png`
is what Claude verified against it. When a feature lands, Claude drops its Tine
shot in and notes exactly which page(s) to screenshot in OG.

Markdown format; `:file/name-format :triple-lowbar` (so `a/b` ⇒ `a___b.md`).

## Examples

| Feature | Pages | What to check | Shots |
|---------|-------|---------------|-------|
| Namespaces (macro + Hierarchy) | `Formula1`, `Formula1/2026`, `Formula1/2026/08 Austrian Grand Prix`, `Formula1/2026/09 Italian Grand Prix`, `Formula1/2025/12 Abu Dhabi Grand Prix` (note: **no `Formula1/2025` page of its own**) | Open **Formula1**: `{{namespace Formula1}}` shows a bold **Namespace** label + the bulleted descendant tree. The automatic **Hierarchy** section lists **one bulleted row per namespace level** as a breadcrumb (`[[Formula1]] / [[2026]] / …`) — including a synthesized **`[[Formula1]] / [[2025]]`** row even though that page has no file. `icon::` is hidden (shown as the title icon, not a property row). | `assets/namespace-tine.png` (Tine); add `assets/namespace-og.png` (real OG) |
| Macros (easy + medium tier) | `Macros` (+ `:macros` in `logseq/config.edn`) | Open **Macros**: `{{twitter}}`=`{{tweet}}`, `{{vimeo id}}`/`{{bilibili BV…}}` iframe embeds, `{{img url w h}}`/`{{img url center}}` sizing+alignment, and **user `:macros`** — `{{poem red, blue}}`→"Roses are red, violets are blue." and `{{hi Martin, Macros}}`→bold + `[[Macros]]` link. `{{youtube-timestamp}}`/`{{cloze}}`/`{{zotero-*}}` render **degraded** (Tine has no YT-player API / SRS / Zotero connector) and say so — expected to differ from OG. | `screenshots/macros.png` (Tine, in repo); add `assets/macros-og.png` (real OG) |

- Race notes for the Abu Dhabi Grand Prix. (Note: no `Formula1/2025` page of its
own — the Hierarchy section must still synthesize a `Formula1 / 2025` level row,
matching OG.)
- Race notes for the Austrian Grand Prix.
- Race notes for the Italian Grand Prix.
- Open this page in **OG** and **Tine** and compare each line.
- Embeds (provider-named macros accept a bare id OR a full URL):
- Degraded (Tine has no on-page YouTube player API / SRS engine / Zotero connector):
- User-defined `:macros` (see `logseq/config.edn`): `$1..$N` filled with comma-separated args, then rendered as markdown:

* Something happened on this day. Let's put something in the agenda 
DEADLINE: <2026-06-26 Fri>
* Tried out the Org demo graph in Tine today

* TODO follow up on the [[kitchen-sink]] feature tour
SCHEDULED: <2026-06-27 Sat>
* DONE loaded the graph and clicked around
* The tree above renders from the {{namespace Formula1}} macro; each page shows its own icon (icon:: / #+ICON:).
* Race notes for the 2023 Bahrain GP.
* Race notes for the 2024 Bahrain GP.

* Welcome — this is an Org-mode demo graph for Tine
Each headline (a line starting with one or more =*=) is a block; this paragraph under the headline is the block's body. Inline styles: *bold*, /italic/, _underline_, +strike+, ~code~, =verbatim=, and ^^highlight^^.
* Links: [[logseq-claude]], a labelled link [[logseq-claude][the project page]], and an external one [[https://orgmode.org][the Org website]]

* Boundary-safe plain text: a/b/c file paths, snake_case_var, and 2*3*4 all stay literal (no accidental emphasis)
id:: 5a54faaf-5e54-45c4-9e09-3cac8a65ef48

* TODO [#A] high-priority task with a schedule
SCHEDULED: <2026-06-26 Fri>
* DOING an in-progress task that references [[n-fold IP]]
* DONE a finished task (note the struck-through marker)
* Inline timestamps: met on <2026-06-26 Fri> (active) and logged [2026-06-20 Sat] (inactive)
* A parent headline — children nest by star depth
* child block with /emphasis/ and a [[logseq-claude]] link

* A plain list inside one block block block
- milk
- eggs
+ also a valid bullet

* An Org table
| Feature  | Status  |
|----------+---------|
| Outliner | done    |
| Queries  | partial |

* An Org source block (a leading * inside stays code, not a headline)
#+BEGIN_SRC clojure
* this line starts with a star but is still source code
(defn hello [] "world")
#+END_SRC

* An Org quote block
#+BEGIN_QUOTE
To be, or not to be, that is the question.
#+END_QUOTE

* A block with a properties drawer
:PROPERTIES:
:key: value
:another: thing
:END:
The drawer above is hidden metadata; this line is the visible body.
* This page exists so the links on [[kitchen-sink]] resolve to real content

* Tine is a fast, Logseq-compatible outliner — see [[kitchen-sink]] for the feature tour
id:: bc03f166-fe8c-4795-b2a3-777da5da10bd
* Anything that links here shows up below in this page's Linked References

* NOTE: This page (and the =mwe-namespace/...= pages) is a Claude-created example demonstrating Logseq namespaces. Safe to delete: remove every file in =pages/= whose name starts with =mwe-namespace=.
* This is a *parent* namespace page. Its name has no slash, but other pages are named under it as =mwe-namespace/<child>=.

* Direct children (Tine shows these in a "Namespace" section below the page; click them):
- [[mwe-namespace/alpha]]
- [[mwe-namespace/beta]]

* A namespace is purely name-based: a page named =a/b/c= is "under" =a/b=. There is no special property — only the =/= in the page name. On disk the =/= is encoded (see the filenames: =mwe-namespace___alpha.org= uses triple-underscore for =/=).
* Try it: open [[mwe-namespace/alpha/deep]] and look at the breadcrumb above its title.
* This is =mwe-namespace/alpha=, a child of [[mwe-namespace]]. (Claude-created example.)
* Above the title you should see a breadcrumb: =mwe-namespace= (clickable, jumps to the parent).
* It has its own child one level deeper: [[mwe-namespace/alpha/deep]].
* This is =mwe-namespace/alpha/deep=, two levels under [[mwe-namespace]]. (Claude-created example.)
* Its breadcrumb should read =mwe-namespace › alpha= (both clickable ancestors).
* This is =mwe-namespace/beta=, a sibling of [[mwe-namespace/alpha]] and a child of [[mwe-namespace]]. (Claude-created example.)
* Unlike alpha, beta has no deeper children — so its "Namespace" section is empty.
* n-fold integer programming — a stub page linked from [[kitchen-sink]]
* It exists so a [[bracket link]] with a space in the name navigates to a real file (=n-fold IP.org=)
